rationale: The main hip roof of this Cedar Hills, UT home shows moderate shadow projection off the north/east eaves onto the ground and lower roof planes, consistent with a 6:12 pitch. The ridge line sits clearly inward from the eave perimeter but is not dramatically compressed, ruling out very shallow (4:12) or very steep (8:12+) pitches. The roof planes are prominently visible from near-nadir, with distinct triangular hip facets and well-defined inter-plane shadow lines, further supporting a mid-range slope. Utah's northern foothill climate (snow loading) and the custom architectural style of this home favor the 5:12–7:12 range, with 6:12 being the most probable given the balanced visual cues. Confidence is moderate due to the complex multi-section hip roof and partial shadow obscuration from tree canopy.
